title: Rethinking young people's aspirations in times of crisis: stories of futures from a de-industrialising city
abstract: In this paper - where we will draw on data from a small scale longitudinal study of young people's post-COVID aspirations and sense of their futures in a de-industrialising city - we will suggest that Appadurai's (2004) ideas about the 'capacity to aspire' encourages us to shift our focus from the 'aspirations' of individual young people to think, instead, about the different resources that might be at play in shaping a capacity to aspire. Departing from the detailed stories of two of the young people who participated in this project, we will argue that critical, post-humanist and futures oriented ontologies offer productive possibilities for reimagining the 'promise of education' in relation to young people's aspirations, their dreams and hopes, fears, anxieties and despair that emerge from the thick of social life (Appadurai, 2004) in which young people live, imagine who they are, and what they might become.
authors_text: Kelly, Peter; Goring, James; Noonan, Meave; Brown, Seth
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
